differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Scapholunate Ligament, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Scapholunate Ligament during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Scapholunate Ligament, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Scapholunate Ligament characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Scapholunate Ligament joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Scapholunate Ligament-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Scapholunate Ligament muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Scapholunate Ligament bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Scapholunate Ligament Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Scapholunate Ligament due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Extensor Carpi Ulnaris Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Extensor Carpi Ulnaris due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: First Dorsal Compartment Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the First Dorsal Compartment tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: First Dorsal Compartment Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the First Dorsal Compartment resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Trigger Thumb Pulley Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Trigger Thumb Pulley joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Trigger Thumb Pulley Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Trigger Thumb Pulley-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Trigger Thumb Pulley Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Trigger Thumb Pulley muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Trigger Thumb Pulley Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Trigger Thumb Pulley bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Trigger Thumb Pulley Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Trigger Thumb Pulley due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Ganglion Cyst tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Ganglion Cyst resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Ganglion Cyst with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Ganglion Cyst, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Ganglion Cyst, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Ganglion Cyst during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Wrist & Hand structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Ganglion Cyst Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Ganglion Cyst, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

name: Cervical Facet Joint Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Cervical Facet Joint bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Facet Joint Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Cervical Facet Joint due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Cervical Disc tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Cervical Disc resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Cervical Disc with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Cervical Disc, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Cervical Disc, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Cervical Disc during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Cervical Disc, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Cervical Disc characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Cervical Disc joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Cervical Disc-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Disc Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Cervical Disc muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Levator Scapulae with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Levator Scapulae, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Levator Scapulae, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Levator Scapulae during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Levator Scapulae, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Levator Scapulae characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Levator Scapulae joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Levator Scapulae-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Levator Scapulae muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Levator Scapulae bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Levator Scapulae Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Levator Scapulae due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Cervical Nerve Root Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Cervical Nerve Root tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.

diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Upper Trapezius during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Upper Trapezius, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Upper Trapezius characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Upper Trapezius joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Upper Trapezius-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Upper Trapezius muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Upper Trapezius bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Upper Trapezius Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Upper Trapezius due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Cervical Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Thoracic Facet Joint Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Thoracic Facet Joint tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Thoracic Facet Joint Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Thoracic Facet Joint resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Thoracic Facet Joint Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Thoracic Facet Joint with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Thoracic Facet Joint Tear (Full-Thickness)
