key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

name: Costovertebral Joint Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Costovertebral Joint joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costovertebral Joint Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Costovertebral Joint-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costovertebral Joint Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Costovertebral Joint muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costovertebral Joint Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Costovertebral Joint bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costovertebral Joint Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Costovertebral Joint due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Costotransverse Joint tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Costotransverse Joint resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Costotransverse Joint with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Costotransverse Joint, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Costotransverse Joint, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Costotransverse Joint during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Costotransverse Joint, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Costotransverse Joint Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Costotransverse Joint characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Thoracic Disc Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Thoracic Disc due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Rhomboid Major tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Rhomboid Major resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Rhomboid Major with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Rhomboid Major, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Rhomboid Major, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Rhomboid Major during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Rhomboid Major, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Rhomboid Major characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Rhomboid Major joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Thoracic Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Rhomboid Major Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Rhomboid Major-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

name: Rhomboid Major Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome

key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Lumbar Facet Joint, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Lumbar Facet Joint, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Lumbar Facet Joint during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Lumbar Facet Joint, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Lumbar Facet Joint characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Lumbar Facet Joint joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Lumbar Facet Joint-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Lumbar Facet Joint muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Lumbar Facet Joint bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Facet Joint Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Lumbar Facet Joint due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Disc Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Lumbar Disc tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Disc Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Lumbar Disc resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Lumbar Nerve Root, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Nerve Root Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Lumbar Nerve Root characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Nerve Root Synovitis
description: Inflammation of the synovial lining of the Lumbar Nerve Root joint.
key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.

treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Nerve Root Nerve Entrapment
description: Compression of the Lumbar Nerve Root-related peripheral nerve causing pain, paraesthesia, and possible motor deficit.
key_findings: Dermatomal/peripheral nerve distribution pain, paraesthesia, weakness in innervated muscles, positive provocation test (e.g., Tinel's, Phalen's).
diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Nerve Root Myofascial Trigger Point Syndrome
description: Hyperirritable spots within the Lumbar Nerve Root muscle producing referred pain and dysfunction.
key_findings: Palpable taut band, jump sign, predictable referred pain pattern, restricted ROM.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate active (refers spontaneously) vs latent (only on palpation). Address perpetuating factors for lasting relief (Travell & Simons).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Nerve Root Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Lumbar Nerve Root bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Lumbar Nerve Root Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Lumbar Nerve Root due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Quadratus Lumborum Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Quadratus Lumborum tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Quadratus Lumborum Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Quadratus Lumborum resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Quadratus Lumborum Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Quadratus Lumborum with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Quadratus Lumborum Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Quadratus Lumborum, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Quadratus Lumborum Bursitis

diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Erector Spinae Stress Fracture
description: Fatigue failure of the Erector Spinae bone due to repetitive submaximal loading.
key_findings: Insidious onset of activity-related pain that progresses to rest pain, focal bony tenderness, possible swelling.
diagnostic_tips: MRI is gold standard (X-ray often negative for first 2-3 weeks). Address training load, nutrition, and biomechanics.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Erector Spinae Avulsion Injury
description: Tearing away of bone fragment at the attachment site of the Erector Spinae due to sudden tensile load.
key_findings: Acute sharp pain at attachment, swelling, weakness on resisted contraction, X-ray shows displaced fragment.
diagnostic_tips: Surgical consult if displacement >2cm. Common sites: ASIS (sartorius), AIIS (rectus femoris), ischial tuberosity (hamstrings).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Tendinopathy
description: Degenerative tendon disorder of the Multifidus tendon characterised by collagen disorganisation, neovascularisation, and load-related pain.
key_findings: Localised tendon pain on loading, morning stiffness <30 min, palpation tenderness over the tendon, often pain-free at rest in early stages.
diagnostic_tips: Distinguish from peritendinitis (crepitus) and partial-thickness tear (focal weakness). Imaging (US/MRI) shows tendon thickening, hypoechoic areas, neovessels.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Strain
description: Acute muscular injury of the Multifidus resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Multifidus with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Multifidus, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Bursitis
description: Inflammation of a bursa adjacent to the Multifidus, producing localised pain and impaired motion.
key_findings: Localised swelling, warmth, tenderness over bursa, painful arc if subacromial.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out septic bursitis (fever, marked redness) — aspiration if suspected. US confirms fluid collection.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Impingement Syndrome
description: Compression and inflammation of soft tissues around the Multifidus during specific movements.
key_findings: Painful arc, positive impingement tests (e.g., Neer/Hawkins for shoulder), pain on end-range loading.
diagnostic_tips: Subacromial impingement is a clinical diagnosis. Consider rotator cuff related shoulder pain (RCRSP) terminology (Lewis 2016).

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Capsulitis
description: Inflammation and progressive fibrosis of the joint capsule around the Multifidus, leading to global motion restriction.
key_findings: Capsular pattern of restriction (e.g., ER>ABD>IR at shoulder), night pain, painful at end-range in all directions.
diagnostic_tips: Self-limiting (12-24 months) but can be shortened with intra-articular corticosteroid + physiotherapy in early phase.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Multifidus Osteoarthritis
description: Degenerative joint disease of the Multifidus characterised by cartilage loss, osteophyte formation, and subchondral bone changes.
key_findings: Activity-related pain, morning stiffness <30 min, crepitus, gradual loss of motion, possible deformity in advanced disease.
diagnostic_tips: Diagnosis is clinical (NICE 2022); imaging only if atypical features. Exercise & weight loss are first-line per OARSI guidelines.

treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.

name: Multifidus Synovitis

key_findings: Joint warmth, swelling, painful active and passive ROM, morning stiffness >30 min if inflammatory.
diagnostic_tips: Rule out infection (joint aspiration if febrile, marked warmth). Rheumatology referral if multiple joints involved.

diagnostic_tips: Confirm with nerve conduction studies. Conservative management first; surgical decompression if severe motor loss or failed conservative care >6 months.

description: Acute muscular injury of the Pars Interarticularis resulting in fibre disruption ranging from microscopic (Grade I) to complete rupture (Grade III).
key_findings: Sudden sharp pain during activity, focal tenderness, pain on stretch and resisted contraction, possible bruising in 24-72h.
diagnostic_tips: Grade by clinical exam: I = pain, no weakness; II = pain + weakness, no full rupture; III = palpable defect, marked weakness. Ultrasound confirms grading.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Pars Interarticularis Tear (Partial-Thickness)
description: Partial-thickness rupture of the Pars Interarticularis with preserved continuity but functional loss.
key_findings: Acute pain at injury, weakness on resisted testing, painful arc if rotator cuff, MRI/US shows partial discontinuity.
diagnostic_tips: Differentiate from full-thickness tear via active vs passive ROM gap and imaging. Conservative management often successful.

treatment_plan.acute: Relative rest from aggravating loads, education on pain neuroscience, ice or heat per symptom response, gentle pain-free ROM, isometric loading at 70% MVIC for analgesia.
treatment_plan.subacute: Progressive loading (HSR/eccentric), motor control retraining, addressing kinetic chain deficits, manual therapy as adjunct, return-to-activity criteria-based progression.
treatment_plan.chronic: Heavy resistance training, plyometric loading if appropriate, sport/work-specific reconditioning, address psychosocial factors with CBT-informed approaches if persistent.

differential_diagnosis: Consider adjacent Lumbar Spine structures, referred pain, inflammatory arthropathies, and red flag pathologies (tumour, infection, fracture).

name: Pars Interarticularis Tear (Full-Thickness)
description: Complete rupture of the Pars Interarticularis, with loss of continuity and significant functional impairment.
causes: Forced eccentric loading on degenerated tissue
key_findings: Sudden pain followed by weakness, palpable defect, marked loss of function, atrophy if chronic.
diagnostic_tips: MRI/US is gold standard. Surgical consult typically warranted, especially in active patients <65 years.
